info.title: Futuur API Documentation
info.description:
> **✨ LATEST VERSION**
> 
> This is the **latest API version (v2.0)**. 
> 
> For reference, the deprecated v1.0 documentation is available at: [https://api.futuur.com/v1.0/docs](https://api.futuur.com/v1.0/docs)

> **🤖 AI Agents Integration**
> 
> For AI agents and automated integrations, access the OpenAPI schema directly at:
> 
> **[https://api.futuur.com/docs/schema/](https://api.futuur.com/docs/schema/)**
> 
> This endpoint provides the complete API specification in JSON format, perfect for code generation, API clients, and AI-powered integrations.


# Introduction

## API Structure

### Events and Markets

The Futuur API is built around **Events** (previously referred to as Questions in older API versions) and **Markets** (previously referred to as Outcomes in older API versions).

- **Events** represent a question or prediction that can be bet on (e.g., "Who will win the 2024 US Presidential Election?")
- **Markets** are the possible outcomes for an event (e.g., "Candidate A", "Candidate B", "Other")

Each event contains multiple markets, and the relationship between markets is defined by **markets correlation**:

- **Linked prices**: Markets are correlated. The sum of all market prices equals 1, and placing a bet on one market automatically affects the prices of other markets in the same event. This is the default correlation type.

- **Independent**: Markets operate independently. Each market's price is calculated separately, and betting on one market does not affect others.

The correlation type determines how prices are calculated and how bets impact market dynamics within an event.

### Limit Orders

Bets are placed through **Limit Orders**, which allow you to specify how you want to participate in a market. Orders can be created in two ways:

- **By Market** (Market Order): Place an order without specifying a price (`price=None`). The order will be executed immediately at the best available price in the order book. You're willing to accept whatever price is currently available for matching your bid (buy) or ask (sell) order.

- **By Price** (Limit Order): Place an order with a specific price you're willing to pay (for buy orders) or receive (for sell orders). The order will be executed when the market price reaches your specified price, or remain in the order book until a matching order is found.

Limit orders provide flexibility in how you enter and exit positions, allowing you to set precise price targets or execute immediately at current market prices.

## Rate Limiting
To ensure the quality and accessibility of our API, we enforce certain rate limits per user.
The limit rates are the following per user:

* **2000 requests / minute**
* **30000 requests / day**

Please be aware that exceeding these limits may result in your access to the API being temporarily suspended.

For more detailed information about the various available endpoints, the expected input parameters, the response
format, error codes to anticipate, and more, please refer to our detailed API documentation or contact our
support team at support@futuur.com.

P.S.: Any attempt to submit the same post request within less than 1 second will result in an 'wager_action_duplicated' error code.

post.description:
Create a new limit order.

Request body (subset):
  - market (pk, required)
  - side: "bid" | "ask" (required)
  - position: "long" | "short" (required, market-dependent)
  - price: decimal string | null (null = market order)
  - shares: decimal string (required)
  - amount: decimal string (optional echo for cost validation)
  - currency: e.g. "OOM", "USDC" (required)
  - expired_at: ISO datetime (optional)
  - cancel_conflicting_orders: bool (default False)

Returns:
  - 201 Created with LimitOrderSerializer data.

Raises:
  - RealMoneyForbiddenCountry, RealMoneyBetNotAllowed
  - EmailNotConfirmed, UserBlockedBets
  - InvalidHMACKey, UserNotEnoughBalance, InvalidShares
  - OutcomeDisabled, MarketClosed / OrderBookMarketClosed
  - OrderBookConflictingOrders, OrderBookInvalidAmount

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional. Unique value chosen by the client for a logical operation. If you repeat the same POST with the same key and the same JSON body after a successful response, the API returns the stored response and does not create another order. A different body (e.g. different price) uses a different fingerprint, so requoting is not blocked.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A unique integer value identifying this limit order.

post.description:
Create multiple limit orders in a single request.

Request body:
  - orders: list[1..20] of:
      - event (int, optional consistency check)
      - market (outcome id, required)
      - side: "bid" | "ask"
      - position: "long" | "short"
      - price: decimal string | null
      - shares: decimal string
      - amount: decimal string (optional)
      - currency: e.g. "OOM", "USDC"
      - expired_at: ISO datetime (optional)
      - cancel_conflicting_orders: bool (optional)

Returns:
  - 200 OK
  - {
      "results": [
        {"index": 0, "success": true, "order": {...}},
        {"index": 1, "success": false, "errors": "..."}
      ],
      "cancelled_existing_order_ids": [1, 2, 3]
    }

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional. Unique value chosen by the client for a logical operation. If you repeat the same POST with the same key and the same JSON body after a successful response, the API returns the stored response and does not create another order. A different body (e.g. different price) uses a different fingerprint, so requoting is not blocked.

post.description:
Batch Cancel Orders.

Request body:
  - order_ids: list[int] (required)

Returns:
  - 200 OK with one result item per requested id:
    [
      {"index": 0, "order_id": 100, "success": true},
      {"index": 1, "order_id": 101, "success": false, "error": "Order is being processed."}
    ]

parameters.name: ordering
parameters.description: Which field to use when ordering the results.

post.description:
Cancel all active limit orders for the authenticated user.

Request body (optional filters):
  - event: int (question id)
  - market: int (outcome id)

Returns:
  - 200 OK
  - {
      "canceled_order_ids": [100, 101],
      "processing_order_ids": [102]
    }

This information helps Market Makers to define its prices policy.
This is purely informative and do not change the markets behavior.

Linked: Indicate that markets are correlated and influence each other.
Independent: Each market is independent from each other. * `linked` - Linked * `independent` - Independent

currencies_with_active_orders.description: List of currencies where the user has at least one active order.

is_wallet_user.description: True if the user signed up via wallet (Privy)

WagerAction.description:
Serializer for WagerAction.
PERFORMANCE TIP: Use select_related('outcome', 'outcome__question', 'outcome__entity', 'wager__user', 'wager')
    on WagerAction queryset.

action.description:
* `sale` - sale
* `partial_sale` - partial sale
* `purchase` - purchase
* `win` - win
* `lose` - lose
* `cancel` - cancel
* `reverse` - reverse
* `disable` - disable

profit.description: User's profit of this action compared with active purchases (latest purchases after any sell)

position.description:
'Long' for bets in favor of the outcome; 'Short' for bets against the outcome

* `long` - long
* `short` - short

* `lost` - lost
* `purchased` - purchased
* `sold` - sold
* `won` - won
* `cancelled` - cancelled
* `disabled` - disabled

* `short` - short

active_purchases.description: purchases from last outcome bought in that question

last_profit.description: Last profit when question is resolved or wager is sold

last_action.description: serialized data about last WagerAction on this wager

actions_count.description: number of actions triggered by user (purchases, sales)

last_sell_amount.description: Last sale amount when wager is sold. This value will always be positive

shares_in_canonical.description: consolidated shares in question's canonical currency

purchases_amount.description: Cost basis of the open position, based on purchases and adjusted by partial sales.

HMAC.name: Key
HMAC.description:
HMAC Authentication requires three headers:

1. **Key** (header): Your PUBLIC KEY
2. **Timestamp** (header): Current timestamp in Unix format
3. **HMAC** (header): SHA-512 hexdigest hash of the request signed with your PRIVATE KEY

The HMAC signature is created from the full raw headers, POST, and GET data of your request, ordered alphabetically by its keys. For example, if making a request to `markets/?category=5`, you must generate a SHA-512 hash from: `category=5&Key={{your_public_key}}&Timestamp={{current_timestamp}}`

See the Authentication section in the API documentation for detailed examples.

x-code-samples.source:
import hashlib
import hmac
import datetime
from collections import OrderedDict
from urllib.parse import urlencode

PRIVATE_KEY = "your_private_key"
PUBLIC_KEY = "your_public_key"

params = {
    "Key": PUBLIC_KEY,
    "Timestamp": int(datetime.datetime.utcnow().timestamp()),
    "category": 5
}

params_to_sign = OrderedDict(sorted(list(params.items())))
params_to_sign = urlencode(params_to_sign)
encoded_params = params_to_sign.encode("utf-8")
encoded_private_key = PRIVATE_KEY.encode("utf-8")
hmac_signature = hmac.new(encoded_private_key, encoded_params, hashlib.sha512).hexdigest()

headers = {
    "Key": PUBLIC_KEY,
    "Timestamp": str(params["Timestamp"]),
    "HMAC": hmac_signature
}

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
### **Authentication**

Most Futuur API endpoints require authentication. Authentication is performed using your API key and is secured by a SHA-512 HMAC.

Each authenticated request must include the following headers:

- **Key**: Your **PUBLIC KEY**
- **Timestamp**: Current timestamp in Unix format
- **HMAC**: SHA-512 hexdigest hash of the request signed with your **PRIVATE KEY**

### **How to Generate the HMAC**

The HMAC signature is created from the full raw headers, POST, and GET data of your request, ordered alphabetically by its keys.

For example, if making a request to `markets/?category=5`, generate a SHA-512 hash from:

```
category=5&Key={{your_public_key}}&Timestamp={{current_timestamp}}
```

### **Python Example**

```python
import hashlib
import hmac
import datetime
from collections import OrderedDict
from urllib.parse import urlencode

PRIVATE_KEY = 'your_private_key'
PUBLIC_KEY = 'your_public_key'

# Prepare parameters
params = {
    'Key': PUBLIC_KEY,
    'Timestamp': int(datetime.datetime.utcnow().timestamp()),
    'category': 5
}

# Sort and encode parameters
params_sorted = OrderedDict(sorted(params.items()))
params_encoded = urlencode(params_sorted).encode('utf-8')

# Generate HMAC signature
hmac_signature = hmac.new(
    PRIVATE_KEY.encode('utf-8'),
    params_encoded,
    hashlib.sha512
).hexdigest()

# Build headers
headers = {
    'Key': PUBLIC_KEY,
    'Timestamp': str(params['Timestamp']),
    'HMAC': hmac_signature
}
```

tags.name: Websocket
tags.description:
Futuur uses Pusher to deliver real-time data to clients over WebSocket connections.

## Authentication and authorization

Two backend endpoints are used:

1. **User authentication** (`POST /pusher/user-auth/`)  
   Identifies the user on the Pusher connection (signin). Send `socket_id` in the body.  
   Use the same DRF authentication as the rest of the API (e.g. `Authorization: Bearer <token>` or HMAC headers).  
   Only authenticated users are allowed.  
   Endpoint is versioned (for example: `POST /v2.0/pusher/user-auth/`).

Python example (HMAC user auth):

API bots can call `POST /pusher/user-auth/` using the same HMAC headers
(`Key`, `Timestamp`, `HMAC`).
To generate these headers, see the **Authentication** section.

Sign the request using:

```
Key={{your_public_key}}&Timestamp={{current_timestamp}}&socket_id={{socket_id}}
```

Python example (user auth):

```python
import hashlib
import hmac
import time
from urllib.parse import urlencode

import requests

API_PUBLIC_KEY = "your_public_key"
API_PRIVATE_KEY = "your_private_key"
USER_AUTH_ENDPOINT = "https://api.futuur.com/v2.0/pusher/user-auth/"


def authenticate_user(socket_id: str) -> dict:
    timestamp = str(int(time.time()))
    params_to_sign = {
        "Key": API_PUBLIC_KEY,
        "Timestamp": timestamp,
        "socket_id": socket_id,
    }
    raw_to_sign = urlencode(sorted(params_to_sign.items()))
    signature = hmac.new(
        API_PRIVATE_KEY.encode("utf-8"),
        raw_to_sign.encode("utf-8"),
        hashlib.sha512,
    ).hexdigest()

    headers = {
        "Key": API_PUBLIC_KEY,
        "Timestamp": timestamp,
        "HMAC": signature,
    }
    payload = {"socket_id": socket_id}
    response = requests.post(USER_AUTH_ENDPOINT, headers=headers, data=payload, timeout=20)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.json()  # {"auth": "...", "user_data": "..."}
```

2. **Channel authorization** (`POST /pusher/auth/`)  
   Authorizes subscription to private channels (e.g. `private-user-{user_id}`).  
   Send `channel_name` and `socket_id` in the body.  
   You can authenticate either with `Authorization: Bearer <token>` (browser/client apps) or with
   HMAC headers (`Key`, `Timestamp`, `HMAC`) for API bots.

### Pusher channel authorization for API bots (HMAC)

For API bots (server-side clients), you can authorize private Pusher channels using
the same HMAC headers (`Key`, `Timestamp`, `HMAC`).
To generate these headers, see the **Authentication** section.

Pusher uses your `channelAuthorization.endpoint` (typically `/pusher/auth`) and sends
`socket_id` and `channel_name`. Your bot must call Futuur API endpoint (for example
`/v2.0/pusher/auth/`) and sign the request with:

```
Key={{your_public_key}}&Timestamp={{current_timestamp}}&channel_name={{channel_name}}&socket_id={{socket_id}}
```

Python example (authorize + subscribe + listen):

```python
import hashlib
import hmac
import json
import ssl
import time
from urllib.parse import urlencode

import certifi
import requests
import websocket

API_PUBLIC_KEY = "your_public_key"
API_PRIVATE_KEY = "your_private_key"
PUSHER_APP_KEY = "0eebda232c11d3fad853"
PUSHER_CLUSTER = "us2"
CHANNEL_AUTH_ENDPOINT = "https://api.futuur.com/v2.0/pusher/auth/"
USER_ID = "1"
CHANNEL_NAME = f"private-user-{USER_ID}"
INSECURE_SSL_FOR_LOCAL_TESTS = False


def sign_headers(socket_id: str, channel_name: str) -> dict:
    timestamp = str(int(time.time()))
    params_to_sign = {
        "Key": API_PUBLIC_KEY,
        "Timestamp": timestamp,
        "socket_id": socket_id,
        "channel_name": channel_name,
    }
    raw_to_sign = urlencode(sorted(params_to_sign.items()))
    signature = hmac.new(
        API_PRIVATE_KEY.encode("utf-8"),
        raw_to_sign.encode("utf-8"),
        hashlib.sha512,
    ).hexdigest()
    return {
        "Key": API_PUBLIC_KEY,
        "Timestamp": timestamp,
        "HMAC": signature,
    }


def authorize_channel(socket_id: str, channel_name: str) -> dict:
    payload = {"socket_id": socket_id, "channel_name": channel_name}
    headers = sign_headers(socket_id, channel_name)
    response = requests.post(
        CHANNEL_AUTH_ENDPOINT,
        headers=headers,
        data=payload,
        timeout=20,
    )
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.json()  # {"auth": "..."}


ws_url = (
    f"wss://ws-{PUSHER_CLUSTER}.pusher.com/app/{PUSHER_APP_KEY}"
    "?protocol=7&client=python-bot&version=1.0&flash=false"
)
sslopt = {"cert_reqs": ssl.CERT_REQUIRED, "ca_certs": certifi.where()}
if INSECURE_SSL_FOR_LOCAL_TESTS:
    # For local debugging only.
    sslopt = {"cert_reqs": ssl.CERT_NONE, "check_hostname": False}

ws = websocket.create_connection(ws_url, timeout=30, sslopt=sslopt)

connection_established = json.loads(ws.recv())
socket_id = json.loads(connection_established["data"])["socket_id"]

auth_data = authorize_channel(socket_id, CHANNEL_NAME)
ws.send(json.dumps({
    "event": "pusher:subscribe",
    "data": {
        "channel": CHANNEL_NAME,
        "auth": auth_data["auth"],
    },
}))

while True:
    # Prints events received from the subscribed channel.
    print(ws.recv())
```

### Authorizing channels other than `private-user-{id}`

`/pusher/auth/` can authorize other private channels too.  
For security, each channel pattern should have specific authorization rules.

Current recommendation:

- `private-user-{id}`: only allow when authenticated user id matches `{id}`
- any additional channel pattern: define explicit permission checks with Futuur dev's
- unknown/private patterns without rules: deny by default

## Client setup (minimal)

Include the Pusher JS client:

```html
<script src="https://js.pusher.com/8.4.0/pusher.min.js"></script>
```

Initialize Pusher and subscribe to a channel:

```html
<script>
    // Enable pusher logging - don't include this in production
    Pusher.logToConsole = true;

    var pusher = new Pusher("9011f7eac38e825792d5", {
        cluster: "us2",
    });

    var channel = pusher.subscribe("my-channel");
    channel.bind("my-event", function (data) {
        alert(JSON.stringify(data));
    });
</script>
```

## Channels and events

Each event below lists the channel name, event name, and data shape emitted to clients.

### Market channel

- **Channel**: `market`
- **Event**: `best-prices-change`
- **Data payload**:
    - `market_id`: int
    - `event_id`: int
    - `currency_mode`: string `play_money` or `real_money`
    - `best_ask`: (number or null)
    - `best_bid`: (number or null)
    - `spread`: `best_ask - best_bid` (number or null)

Example subscription:

```html
<script>
    var pusher = new Pusher("9011f7eac38e825792d5", { cluster: "us2" });
    var channel = pusher.subscribe("market");
    channel.bind("best-prices-change", function (data) {
        console.log("Best prices changed:", data);
    });
</script>
```

### Market channel: full-depth order book (`order-book-update`)

Real-time streaming of the full order book (all price levels with depth) on the public `market` channel. Used for market making: adjust quotes from depth, detect execution risk, and size orders.

- **Channel**: `market` (public, no auth)
- **Event**: `order-book-update`
- **When**: book changes (orders filled, canceled, or new orders); first update per (market, currency, side) after cache miss is a full snapshot.

#### Payload

| Field           | Type    | Description                                                                  |
| --------------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `market_id`     | number  | Market (outcome) ID                                                          |
| `event_id`      | number  | Event (question) ID                                                          |
| `currency_mode` | string  | `'real_money'` or `'play_money'`                                             |
| `side`          | string  | `'bid'` or `'ask'` (side of the book being updated)                          |
| `updates`       | array   | Delta or full levels (see below)                                             |
| `timestamp`     | string  | ISO 8601 with milliseconds                                                   |
| `is_snapshot`   | boolean | If `true`, `updates` is the full book for this side; otherwise it is a delta |

Each element of `updates`:

| Field          | Type   | Description                                                 |
| -------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `price`        | number | Price level (e.g. in USDC)                                  |
| `total_shares` | number | Total shares at this level; `0` means the level was removed |
| `total_amount` | number | Total value (`price * total_shares`) at this level          |

- **Snapshot**: on first update after backend restart or when client (re)subscribes, request a full book via the REST order-book API; the backend may also send `is_snapshot: true` with the full list of levels.
- **Delta**: only levels that changed are sent; `total_shares: 0` indicates a level removed.

Example:

```javascript
channel.bind("order-book-update", function (data) {
    if (data.is_snapshot) {
        // Replace local book for this market/side/currency
        applySnapshot(
            data.market_id,
            data.side,
            data.currency_mode,
            data.updates,
        );
    } else {
        applyDelta(data.market_id, data.side, data.currency_mode, data.updates);
    }
});
```

On (re)connection, request a fresh snapshot from the REST API for the markets you subscribe to, then apply subsequent `order-book-update` deltas.

## Private channel: order updates (hedge / Liquid Loop)

Real-time order status change events for low-latency hedge reactions (<100ms from fill/cancel to push). Covers all status transitions so the Liquid Loop can keep its internal order model and hedge map in sync (no stale open orders).

- **Channel**: `private-user-{user_id}` (requires authentication via `POST /pusher/auth/`)
- **Event**: `order-update`
- **When**: order status changes: filled, partial_filled, canceled, or expired (any transition from open/partial_filled)

### Payload (OrderUpdateWebSocketEvent)

| Field                 | Type           | Description                                                                                                                                           |
| --------------------- | -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `order_id`            | number         | ID da ordem no Futuur                                                                                                                                 |
| `market_id`           | number         | ID do mercado (outcome)                                                                                                                               |
| `event_id`            | number         | ID do evento (question) para hedge lookup                                                                                                             |
| `status`              | string         | `'pending'` \| `'open'` \| `'partial_filled'` \| `'filled'` \| `'canceled'` \| `'expired'`                                                            |
| `previous_status`     | string \| null | Status anterior (para rastrear transição)                                                                                                             |
| `side`                | string         | `'bid'` \| `'ask'`                                                                                                                                    |
| `position`            | string         | `'l'` (long) \| `'s'` (short)                                                                                                                         |
| `price`               | number \| null | Preço limite da ordem                                                                                                                                 |
| `shares_requested`    | number         | Quantidade solicitada                                                                                                                                 |
| `shares_filled`       | number         | Quantidade total preenchida até agora                                                                                                                 |
| `shares_filled_delta` | number         | _(Opcional)_ Preenchido nesta atualização (em eventos de fill)                                                                                        |
| `avg_fill_price`      | number \| null | _(Opcional)_ Preço médio dos fills (em eventos de fill)                                                                                               |
| `currency`            | string         | Código da moeda (ex: USDC)                                                                                                                            |
| `timestamp`           | string         | ISO 8601 com milissegundos                                                                                                                            |
| `cancel_reason`       | string         | _(Opcional)_ `'user_requested'` \| `'market_closed'` \| `'expired'` \| `'system'` \| `'insufficient_funds'` \| `'admin'` (apenas em canceled/expired) |
| `expired_at`          | string         | _(Opcional)_ Timestamp de expiração (ISO 8601)                                                                                                        |
| `is_system_initiated` | boolean        | _(Opcional)_ `true` se o cancelamento foi iniciado pelo sistema                                                                                       |

### Example: subscribe to private order updates

```html
<script>
    var pusher = new Pusher("9011f7eac38e825792d5", { cluster: "us2" });
    var privateChannel = pusher.subscribe("private-user-" + userId);
    privateChannel.bind("order-update", function (data) {
        console.log("Order update:", data);
        if (data.status === "filled" || data.status === "partial_filled") {
            // Trigger hedge (e.g. Liquid Loop)
        }
        if (data.status === "canceled" || data.status === "expired") {
            // Remove from local open-orders model and hedge map
        }
    });
</script>
```

Authentication: the client must call `POST /pusher/auth/` with `channel_name` and `socket_id`.
Use either `Authorization: Bearer <token>` or HMAC headers (`Key`, `Timestamp`, `HMAC`).
The backend validates the user and allows only `private-user-{user_id}` for that user.

Other private channel patterns can also be authorized, but each pattern must have explicit backend permission rules.
